skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro (Black Forest Labs' precise local image-edit model) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model.
skill.summary: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro using precise local editing and strong prompt control
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 53 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is documentation for a legitimate AI image editing service via CLI. Backtick patterns are markdown syntax in code blocks. Hardcoded URLs point to the service's own documentation. Path traversal patterns are placeholder examples. Hidden file references are legitimate token storage paths. No confirmed security risks found.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged path traversal patterns at lines 70, 81. These are placeholder examples in documentation JSON schemas (<absolute/path>, https://...) showing expected format, not actual path traversal attacks.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are clearly placeholder examples in documentation code blocks showing JSON schema format, not malicious path traversal attempts.

high_findings.title: Hidden File Access - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json at line 163 as hidden file access. This is legitimate POSIX configuration file storage for the API token. Normal and expected behavior for CLI tools.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json is standard POSIX convention for CLI configuration files. No malicious hidden file access present.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged product names as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' at lines 4, 109, 144. These lines contain displayName and product comparison tables with no cryptographic code present.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching error - lines contain product names like 'Flux Kontext Pro' and table data. No cryptographic algorithms present in this documentation file.

medium_findings.title: External Commands (Backticks) - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 36 backtick occurrences as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code block delimiters (```) and inline code syntax in documentation, not actual shell command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backticks appear in fenced code blocks (```) or inline code (`) as markdown documentation syntax, not as actual shell command execution delimiters.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged URLs as 'Hardcoded URL'. All URLs point to runcomfy.com domains (service's own documentation and website). These are legitimate reference links, not malicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs are legitimate service documentation links to runcomfy.com domains. No malicious endpoint connections detected.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Nano Banana Edit / GPT Image 2 edit / Flux 2 Klein' at line 41 as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate product names for AI image editing models, not reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Flagged text is a product comparison table listing competitor AI image editing services. Standard documentation practice for model selection guidance.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux Kontext Pro
content.value_statement: AI image editing often produces inconsistent results with generic prompts. This skill bundles Flux Kontext Pro's documented prompting patterns to deliver precise local edits with strong prompt control and consistent high-fidelity outputs.
actual_capabilities: Single-image precise local edits with prompt control
High-fidelity preservation of source identity through targeted changes
Brand asset text and color swaps with quoted preservation instructions
Quick iteration on image modifications with seed-based reproducibility
CLI-based image editing via RunComfy Model API
limitations: Single source image only - no multi-image fanout capabilities
Compound prompts can cause drift - requires sequential single-instruction passes
Less reliable for multilingual or embedded text editing compared to GPT Image 2
Public RunComfy documentation is minimal - model page may have additional fields

use_cases.title: Brand Asset Editing
use_cases.description: Swap text on product labels, change brand colors, or modify specific elements while preserving the overall product appearance. Ideal for marketing teams needing consistent brand asset updates.

use_cases.title: Portrait Micro-Adjustments
use_cases.description: Add accessories, change clothing details, or modify expressions while keeping the subject's identity intact. Perfect for photographers and content creators refining portrait shots.

use_cases.title: Product Image Variations
use_cases.description: Generate reproducible product variations using seed control. Create series of product shots with specific changes for A/B testing or catalog variations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Local Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image: [describe the change you want]. Keep [elements to preserve] unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic single-change edit to an existing image

prompt_templates.title: Preservation-Led Brand Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the [product silhouette], [background], and [lighting] exactly as in the input. Replace [specific element] from [old value] to [new value]. Same [style details].
prompt_templates.scenario: Brand asset modification requiring element preservation

prompt_templates.title: Seed-Controlled Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image with seed [number]: [describe change]. Keep [elements] unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating reproducible variants for comparison or batch processing

prompt_templates.title: Sequential Compound Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Pass 1: [first change instruction]. Pass 2: [second change instruction based on Pass 1 result].
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex edits split into sequential single-instruction passes to avoid drift

output_examples.input: Add a leather shoulder bag, dark brown, hanging on the right shoulder. Keep the person's face, pose, and clothing unchanged.
output_examples.output: A portrait image with the person now wearing a dark brown leather shoulder bag on the right shoulder, with face, pose, and original clothing preserved.

output_examples.input: Keep the bottle silhouette, table, and lighting exactly as in the input. Replace only the brand text on the label, from ALPHA to AURA. Same font weight, white on black, centered.
output_examples.output: A product image of a bottle with the label text changed from ALPHA to AURA, with all other elements (bottle shape, table, lighting) preserved exactly.

output_examples.input: Add an orange umbrella in her left hand. Keep the person's face, pose, and clothing unchanged.
output_examples.output: A portrait image showing the same person holding an orange umbrella in their left hand, with face, pose, and clothing unchanged.

best_practices: Lead with preservation instructions: 'Keep [elements] unchanged' before stating the change for better identity preservation
Use declarative mood in prompts: 'She is now holding X' rather than imperative 'Add X to her'
Split compound edits into sequential passes if the model drifts - pass 1: background, pass 2: clothing changes
anti_patterns: Compound prompts trying to change multiple elements at once - causes drift and inconsistent results
Passing multiple source images - Flux Kontext uses single-ref only, use Nano Banana Edit for multi-image flows
Passive voice prompts like 'should be changed to' instead of declarative statements like 'is now [value]'

faq.question: What is Flux Kontext Pro?
faq.answer: Flux 1 Kontext Pro is Black Forest Labs' precise local image-edit model. It excels at single-image edits where you need to change specific elements while preserving the overall composition and identity.

faq.question: How does this skill work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: The skill triggers when you mention flux kontext, flux-kontext, kontext, or BFL kontext. It invokes the RunComfy CLI to call the Flux Kontext Pro model via the blackforestlabs/flux-1-kontext/pro/edit endpoint.

faq.question: What can I edit with Flux Kontext?
faq.answer: You can do precise local edits like adding objects, changing clothing, swapping text on labels, or modifying colors. It preserves source identity well for targeted changes.

faq.question: What are the limitations?
faq.answer: Flux Kontext works with a single source image only. For multi-image edits, use Nano Banana Edit. For text in images, GPT Image 2 edit often works better. Compound prompts can drift - split them into sequential passes.

faq.question: How do I get reproducible results?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter with an integer value. Reusing the same seed lets you generate variants for comparison or create consistent batch edits.

faq.question: Is my image data secure?
faq.answer: Image URLs are fetched by the RunComfy model server, not by the CLI on your machine. Your API token is stored in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. Set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var in CI environments for container security.
